north_star_verdict.headline: Live, engaged Shortlisted deal, but a fresh 29 Jun Procurement-only RFP (decision due 16 Aug) makes the 31 Aug close date implausible.
north_star_verdict.math: Today 6 Jul 2026; close 31 Aug (56 days out). In Shortlisted since 13 May (~8 weeks); in the deal since 13 Mar. Manscaped re-issued an RFP on 29 Jun (Procurement-Only vs P2P), responses due 16 Jul, decision due 16 Aug. A 16 Aug award leaves only 15 days for pricing finalisation, budget sign-off, MSA/SOW, InfoSec and NetSuite integration scoping before 31 Aug. Buyer is two-way engaged (incoming email 16 Jun; buyer-issued RFP 29 Jun), so the deal is healthy; the ONLY problem is the date, which realistically slips to mid/late September.
north_star_verdict.recommended_forecast: Upside / Pipeline for the 31 Aug date (not Commit); realistic close is late September given the 16 Aug decision and unscoped contracting.
evidence: Next Step 29 Jun: buyer issued a new RFP, Procurement-Only vs P2P, responses due 16 Jul, decision due 16 Aug.
Buyer incoming email received 16 Jun confirms live two-way engagement.
Vatricia McClurg confirmed a mid-June decision target and a 3-6 month go-live on the 12 May RFP/Discovery call.
No economic buyer, MSA/SOW, InfoSec or NetSuite integration scoping evidenced in any source; only 15 days sit between the 16 Aug decision and the 31 Aug close.

metrics.narrative: No quantified value case has been articulated by the buyer across the three read calls. The pain is clear but qualitative: on 12 May 2026 Vatricia McClurg said her team is 'managing a lot in our emails, and then everybody is at different paces' and flagged tail-spend leakage where 'someone has committed... gotten commitment from a supplier... without any approval flow'. Becca West (1 Apr 2026) cited Coupa's clunky NetSuite integration and a UI where 'the PO isn't tied to the invoice'. None of this has been converted into dollars, hours, or cycle-time targets, and the Coupa spend baseline is unknown. The move is to co-author a quantified tail-spend and Coupa-switch savings model with Becca before the 16 Jul RFP response so the business case is explicit.

economic_buyer.narrative: No economic buyer has surfaced on any call. All four contacts are mid-level and procurement/systems-focused: Anita Newsom (procurement lead and referral source), Becca West (Director of Financial and Accounting Systems), Lisa Miller (Purchasing Manager), and Vatricia McClurg (Procurement Specialist running the day-to-day eval). At $130k a director-level group can often approve, but no budget owner or signatory has been named or engaged and the approval chain is undocumented. Becca West's finance-systems seat is the closest lead to budget authority evidenced so far. The move is for the owner to ask Vatricia directly who owns the procurement budget and signs, and to secure a finance introduction before the 16 Aug decision.
sources: SF contact roles (Lisa Miller, Becca West)

decision_criteria.narrative: The evaluation criteria are explicit and buyer-stated. Vatricia McClurg (12 May 2026) named them: a centralised procurement hub for RFP/RFQ/RFI, supplier onboarding with document storage, NDA gating before RFP submission, bid comparison/analysis, and the ability to 'track POs after an invoice date, but not the ability to necessarily enter POs'. Two integrations are hard requirements, confirmed repeatedly: NetSuite (their ERP, staying) and LinkSquares (their CLM). Simplicity is a stated criterion, 'we aren't looking for something that's too big or complex for us'. On 22 May Lisa Miller and Vatricia praised the Teams/Merlin intake workflow and supplier-risk visibility. AI is a 'nice to have', not a criterion (12 May: 'we are interested in it... but most importantly is the basics').

decision_process.narrative: The process is reconstructable from the call and email sequence. It began P2P (procurement + AP) driven by Lisa Miller and Becca West on an AP/procurement RFP; on 28 Apr Manscaped excluded Zycus from the AP track but kept us in a procurement RFP. Vatricia McClurg (who joined Manscaped ~Apr) took over the procurement evaluation on 12 May, ran the 22 May demo, then on 1 Jun declined further downstream demos ('we will not need to schedule a demo for the downstream modules... we are only pursuing upstream modules') and said they were deciding internally. On 29 Jun the buyer formalised a new Procurement-Only vs P2P RFP: responses due 16 Jul, decision due 16 Aug. The approval chain above Vatricia is undocumented, and the shift to a formal RFP re-opens a competitive/committee process rather than a sole-source close.
sources: SF Next Step history 28 Apr / 1 Jun / 29 Jun 2026

paper_process.narrative: Contracting mechanics are mostly unknown, though an NDA is already executed: the LinkSquares-signed 'Zycus Manscaped NDA' was completed and returned by 8 May 2026 (email tasks 8-21 May), so the NDA gate Becca West referenced on 1 Apr ('if we move further... and we have an NDA signed, we can share that') is cleared. Beyond that there is no evidence of an MSA/SOW path, legal review, procurement/vendor-onboarding channel, or InfoSec/security review. The incumbent Coupa renewal is the likely forcing function but its expiry date is not on record. Visibility requires asking Vatricia for the post-16 Aug paper path now, since only 15 days separate the decision from the 31 Aug close.

identify_pain.narrative: The pain is specific, confirmed, and owned by procurement. Vatricia McClurg (12 May 2026) owns the process pain: a manual, email-and-document-driven procurement function with no centralised hub, worsening as the team scales into indirect spend, plus tail-spend leakage from off-process commitments ('downstream, we're seeing that invoice. We don't know who that is, where it's come from... without any approval flow'). Becca West (1 Apr) owns the systems pain: Coupa's poor UI, PO not tied to invoice across multiple screens, and clunky NetSuite integration. The 22 May demo reinforced 'digital fragmentation' across disconnected tools. The compelling event (inferred, not stated) is the Coupa renewal. Pain owner is the growing procurement function under Anita Newsom and Vatricia McClurg.

champion.narrative: Anita Newsom is the entry champion: she recommended and onboarded Zycus at Ventura Foods and a prior construction company, reconnected with Zycus at ProcureCon, and made the introduction ('I recommended and onboarded Zycus at Ventura Foods... it kind of reminded me of you all', 1 Apr 2026). That is real advocacy and is why Zycus was pulled into the procurement RFP. What is missing: she has not appeared on a call since 1 Apr (~13 weeks) and is absent from recent activity; day-to-day ownership sits with Vatricia McClurg, who is engaged and gave warm demo feedback ('the Teams integration is great... it looks really integrative... customizable', 22 May) but reads as a neutral evaluator, not an advocate, and is running a competitive RFP. The move is to re-activate Anita to coach the RFP scoring and convert Vatricia into an active internal champion.

competition.narrative: Coupa is the incumbent being evaluated for displacement on renewal; Becca West (1 Apr 2026) said 'we're on Coupa' and handed us the wedge: poor UI, PO not tied to invoice, and a clunky NetSuite integration. The second, genuinely live threat is inertia, 'Do Nothing / stay on Coupa': on 1 Apr Becca framed it as 'do we wanna stay with our vendor? Do we wanna maybe move', and the 29 Jun re-scoped RFP plus the 1 Jun narrowing signal a defensive, cost-cautious posture. LinkSquares is the buyer's CLM and an integration requirement, not a procurement rival (dormant). No other procurement vendor is named in any source. We win on the integration-and-simplicity wedge (NetSuite + LinkSquares + Teams, midsize-appropriate) where Coupa frustrates them, and by making the Coupa renewal an expensive status quo.

deal_movement.summary: Two-way engaged but the shape is narrowing: after a well-received 22 May demo, the buyer declined further downstream demos (1 Jun), re-cut pricing to procurement-only (9 Jun), and on 29 Jun issued a fresh Procurement-Only vs P2P RFP (responses 16 Jul, decision 16 Aug). Close date pushed twice, 30 Jun -> 30 Jul -> 31 Aug. Verdict holds at Close Date Risk.

items.change: Buyer issued a new Procurement-Only vs P2P RFP; responses due 16 Jul, decision due 16 Aug.
Close date pushed 30 Jul -> 31 Aug by the deal owner.
Forecast category moved Best Case -> Upside Key Deal by Michael McCarthy.
Updated procurement-only pricing shared after buyer confirmed upstream-only scope.
Buyer declined further downstream-module demos and moved to internal decision.
22 May procurement demo landed well (Vatricia and Lisa praised Teams/Merlin intake and supplier-risk views).

competitive_position.summary: Coupa is the incumbent being displaced on renewal and remains the single strongest CURRENT threat, its wedge recency-confirmed (poor UI, PO not tied to invoice, clunky NetSuite integration, Becca West 1 Apr). The live second threat is inertia / 'Do Nothing / stay on Coupa', reinforced by the 29 Jun scope-narrowing to procurement-only. LinkSquares is a CLM integration requirement, not a procurement rival (dormant). No other procurement vendor is named in any source.

competitors.name: Coupa
competitors.quote: we're on Coupa... the UI is probably the main struggle... the PO isn't tied to the invoice... the connection with Netsuite is clunky at best
competitors.how_we_win: Lean on the AI-native, single-code-base platform with clean NetSuite + LinkSquares + Teams integration and a midsize-appropriate simple UI; quantify the tail-spend and switching savings Coupa is not delivering.

competitors.name: Do Nothing / stay on Coupa
competitors.quote: do we wanna stay with our vendor? Do we wanna maybe move, and kind of starting that process
competitors.source: Avoma intro call 1 Apr 2026 (Becca West); SF Next Step 29 Jun 2026 (scope narrowed to Procurement-Only)
competitors.how_we_win: Make the Coupa renewal a forcing event; sharpen the procurement-only price and quantify the cost of staying fragmented so inertia becomes the expensive option.

competitors.name: LinkSquares
competitors.quote: we do use Linksquares... do you guys integrate with that?
competitors.how_we_win: Integrate cleanly with LinkSquares as required; position Zycus iContract only as a future consolidation option, never a rip-and-replace now.

customer_expectations_fit.summary: Strong fit on the core procurement ask (centralised RFP/supplier hub, NetSuite + LinkSquares + Teams integration, simplicity); exposed on AP/payments (out of scope by buyer choice) and unverified on supplier quality/spec-document storage.

items.criterion: Centralised RFP/RFQ/supplier hub to replace email-and-document sprawl
items.quote: we would like kind of a centralized place to have all those items so then tracking downstream is a little bit easier

items.criterion: NetSuite ERP and LinkSquares CLM integration
items.quote: do you guys integrate with Netsuite... we do use Linksquares
we aren't looking for something that's too big or complex for us
is there an opportunity to store documents, like quality documents and specification documents in that module?
items.source: Avoma intro call 1 Apr 2026 (Anita Newsom); Zycus to confirm

items.quote: we have decided to not move Zycus forward in our RFP process for AP

items.requirement: Complete and submit the Procurement RFP (3-tab Excel) by 16 Jul.
items.quote: Attached you will find the RFP excel document which contains 3 tabs, please complete the highlighted fields

items.requirement: Provide a per-module feature breakdown to reference against the demo.
items.quote: is there anywhere... that only lists out what features per module... that we can reference when we're doing our review?
items.source: Avoma demo 22 May 2026; delivered with hyperlinked pricing 27 May

items.deliverable: Communicate the Procurement RFP outcome and next steps after the 16 Aug decision.
items.grounding_quote: We are reviewing internally and I will update you on next steps.
items.source: SF email task 1 Jun 2026 (Vatricia McClurg); Next Step 29 Jun 2026

items.area: Economic buyer / budget owner and approval chain
items.quote: No budget owner or signatory named on any call; Vatricia is a Procurement Specialist.

items.area: Quantified savings / ROI baseline (Coupa spend, tail-spend value)
items.quote: Pain stated but never quantified in dollars, hours, or cycle time.

items.area: Post-decision paper path (MSA/SOW, InfoSec, procurement channel)
items.quote: NDA signed but no MSA/SOW or InfoSec path evidenced.

best_practice_check.summary: Win the procurement beachhead now, multi-thread to a budget owner, and make the Coupa renewal the forcing event.
flags: Scope narrowed P2P -> Procurement-only (defensive cost-cut); win the beachhead by nailing the 16 Jul RFP and simple pricing, then phase eInvoicing/AP later.
Single real thread is a passive champion: Anita Newsom off calls since 1 Apr while junior Vatricia McClurg runs the eval; multi-thread up to the finance budget owner before 16 Aug.
Primary threat is inertia and the Coupa renewal, not a new rival; quantify the cost of staying fragmented so 'Do Nothing' is the expensive option.

items.name: Vatricia McClurg
items.title: Procurement Specialist
items.risk: Runs the competitive RFP and owns day-to-day; engaged but not yet an advocate and relatively junior. Convert to champion and map who she reports to on budget.
items.source: SF email tasks + Avoma calls 12 May / 22 May 2026

items.name: Anita Newsom
items.title: Procurement lead (referral source)
items.risk: Genuine prior advocate (Zycus at Ventura Foods) but off calls ~13 weeks; advocacy may fade. Re-activate to coach the RFP.
items.source: Avoma intro call 1 Apr 2026; SF task contact

items.name: Becca West
items.title: Financial and Accounting Systems Director
items.risk: Named the Coupa pain and closest lead to budget authority; keep engaged on integration fit and enlist for the ROI model.
items.source: SF contact role; Avoma intro call 1 Apr 2026

items.name: Lisa Miller
items.title: Purchasing Manager
items.risk: Drove the AP track where Zycus was excluded; ensure procurement scope stays primary.
items.source: SF contact role; SF email task 28 Apr 2026

champion_strength.summary: Anita Newsom is a genuine but currently passive champion (referral source, prior Zycus customer at Ventura Foods) who has been off calls since 1 Apr 2026; day-to-day ownership sits with neutral evaluator Vatricia McClurg. Net strength is developing and at risk.
champion_strength.source: Avoma intro call 1 Apr 2026; SF activity history

ai_positioning_strength.summary: Zycus led hard with Merlin agentic intake and the autonomous negotiation agent across both demos, but the buyer stayed focused on the basics (centralisation, RFP workflow, integration) and treated AI as a nice-to-have. The agentic story is presented, not landed; it is Zycus-driven, not buyer-pulled.

ai_fit_signal.summary: AIS field reads 4.0 / Moderate. Calls corroborate rather than raise it: Vatricia (12 May) said 'we are interested in it... but most importantly is the basics', and the demos generated no buyer-initiated AI requirements. AI is a differentiator we push, not a stated buying criterion.

items.detail: Scope narrowed P2P -> Procurement-only with no economic buyer engaged; a defensive, cost-cautious posture that drags win confidence.
No budget owner or signatory named or engaged; the eval is run by a junior Procurement Specialist with the senior champion off calls.
16 Aug decision leaves only 15 days for pricing, sign-off, MSA/SOW and NetSuite integration scoping before the 31 Aug close; contracting path unscoped.
'Do Nothing / stay on Coupa' is a live path; buyer inertia and a Coupa renewal could lock them in for another cycle.
items.source: Avoma intro call 1 Apr 2026; SF Next Step 29 Jun 2026

confidence_signals.summary: Engaged but cooling in shape: warm demo, then declined further demos, narrowed scope, and reverted to a formal RFP rather than a direct decision. Two-way contact is recent (16 Jun) but the trend is defensive.
items: Buyer declined further downstream demos and moved to internal decision (1 Jun).
Scope narrowed to procurement-only and a fresh RFP issued (29 Jun).
Senior champion Anita Newsom off calls since 1 Apr; junior owner runs the eval.

critical_signals.text: The real fight is displacing incumbent Coupa on renewal, and the wedge is confirmed: poor UI, PO not tied to invoice, and clunky NetSuite integration named by Becca West on 1 Apr.
Manscaped narrowed the deal from full P2P to procurement-only and issued a fresh RFP on 29 Jun with a decision due 16 Aug, a defensive scope cut that resets the process.

The 22 May procurement demo landed well (Vatricia praised the Teams integration and called the platform integrative and customizable), but the buyer then declined further downstream demos and went internal.
Pricing was re-cut to procurement-only on 9 Jun and the rep offered to get competitive, but no budget owner has validated it and no ROI baseline is quantified.

Vatricia McClurg, a Procurement Specialist who joined Manscaped this spring, now runs the evaluation day-to-day; engaged but neutral, while senior champion Anita Newsom has been off calls since 1 Apr.

scope_change.detail: Zycus was excluded from the AP RFP on 28 Apr; the buyer confirmed 'we are only pursuing more upstream modules' on 1 Jun, pricing was re-cut to procurement-only on 9 Jun, and a formal Procurement-Only vs P2P RFP was issued 29 Jun. Signals a defensive, cost-cautious posture and likely a phased rather than big-bang buy.

deal_scores_evidence.summary: A live, well-engaged Coupa-displacement in Shortlisted where the demo landed, but the buyer just narrowed scope to procurement-only and reverted to a formal RFP (decision 16 Aug) with no economic buyer engaged, so the win is real but capped by a passive champion and a slipping 31 Aug date.

win_position.text: Why this number: Shortlisted caps confidence in the low-60s; we earn it on a confirmed Coupa wedge (UI, PO-not-tied-to-invoice, clunky NetSuite integration, Becca West 1 Apr) and a warm 22 May demo, but hold below because the buyer narrowed scope and no budget owner is engaged.

We are in the final procurement RFP against the incumbent, entered via a genuine referral (Anita Newsom used Zycus at Ventura Foods) and integrate cleanly with NetSuite and LinkSquares, the two named must-haves.
Risk: the 29 Jun narrowing from P2P to procurement-only plus a reverted formal RFP is a defensive, cost-cautious signal, not a lean toward us, and 'Do Nothing / stay on Coupa' remains a live path.
Risk: the eval is run by a junior Procurement Specialist (Vatricia McClurg) with the senior champion Anita Newsom off calls since 1 Apr and no economic buyer identified, so power access is thin.

deal_momentum.text: Two-way engaged recently: buyer email 16 Jun and a buyer-issued RFP 29 Jun with concrete dates (responses 16 Jul, decision 16 Aug, 3-6 month go-live).

Momentum is softening in shape: after the 22 May demo the buyer declined further downstream demos (1 Jun) and reverted from a direct decision to a formal RFP, and the close date has slipped twice to 31 Aug.

customer_commitment.text: Commitment signals are real but mid-level: NDA executed by 8 May, procurement-only pricing requested and shared, and Vatricia confirmed they need no further demo before deciding.
No economic-buyer meeting, no signed mutual close plan, and no quantified business case; commitment is procedural (an RFP) rather than a decisive lean to Zycus.

deal_risk.text: The 16 Aug decision leaves only 15 days for pricing, sign-off, MSA/SOW and integration scoping before 31 Aug, so the date will likely slip to September.
Scope-shrink plus an unengaged budget owner and a passive champion are the main risks; the competitive threat is inertia and the Coupa renewal rather than a new rival.

scope_shrink.evidence: Narrowed P2P -> procurement-only and reverted to a formal RFP on 29 Jun (defensive).

economic_buyer_engaged.evidence: No budget owner named or engaged; eval run by a junior Procurement Specialist.

champion_active.evidence: Champion Anita Newsom off calls since 1 Apr; day-to-day owner is a neutral evaluator.

customer_preference.evidence: Warm demo feedback ('the Teams integration is great... it looks really integrative... customizable', Vatricia 22 May) and Zycus kept in the procurement RFP, but no decisive Zycus-specific lean and scope was narrowed rather than expanded.

business_case.evidence: Qualitative pain only (email/document sprawl, tail-spend leakage, Coupa UI/integration frustration); no savings, hours, or cycle-time quantified.

items.action: Submit a tailored procurement-only RFP response by 16 Jul leading with the Coupa wedge.
items.trigger: Buyer issued a Procurement-Only vs P2P RFP, responses due 16 Jul.
items.expected_effect: Keeps Zycus in the final selection and frames NetSuite/LinkSquares/Teams integration and simplicity against Coupa's known gaps.

items.action: Re-engage Anita Newsom to sponsor and coach the RFP scoring.
items.trigger: Champion off calls since 1 Apr while a competitive RFP runs.
items.expected_effect: Restores internal advocacy and reduces the risk of a neutral evaluation defaulting to the incumbent.

items.action: Ask Vatricia who owns the procurement budget and signs, and request a finance intro before 16 Aug.
items.trigger: No economic buyer identified on any call.
items.expected_effect: Maps power to the budget owner so pricing and sign-off can clear before the decision date.

items.action: Co-author a quantified tail-spend and Coupa-switch savings model with Becca West.
items.trigger: Pain is stated but never quantified; do-nothing is a live path.
items.expected_effect: Turns qualitative pain into a defensible ROI so inertia and the Coupa renewal become the expensive option.

items.action: Confirm the post-16 Aug paper path (MSA/SOW, InfoSec, NetSuite scoping) with Vatricia.
items.trigger: Only 15 days sit between the 16 Aug decision and the 31 Aug close.
items.expected_effect: Protects the close date by scoping contracting early or resetting the forecast to a credible September date.

gaps: Economic buyer / budget owner unnamed on this opportunity.
Anita Newsom's exact Salesforce title not on record (referral source, leads procurement).
No MSA/SOW, InfoSec, or post-decision contracting path evidenced.
No quantified savings/ROI baseline (Coupa spend unknown).
